summary: A solid sound‑board recording of the August 1971 Bronx show, bursting with a scorching Hard to Handle and a magical Empty Pages.
review: The Charlie Miller/Rolfe SBD delivers a clear, well‑balanced picture of this historic August ’71 gig despite occasional tape‑warble hiss.  Performances are high‑energy – Pigpen shines on Hard to Handle, Empty Pages and the first set classics, while the second set rolls through a ripping Bertha, St. Stephen > Not Fade Away > Goin’ Down the Road sequence.  Overall the show is celebrated as one of Pig’s last great outings and a must‑listen for any 1971 enthusiast.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ≈ 4.4, overwhelmingly positive comments about performance and a consistently solid soundboard source; minor tape quirks keep it from “excellent”.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced SBD from Charlie Miller, with occasional hiss/tape‑warble noted but not disruptive; minor sonic anomalies mentioned by a few reviewers.

standout_songs: St. Stephen > Not Fade Away > Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set praised for strong Pigpen‑heavy selection (Hard to Handle, Good Lovin', Sugar Magnolia); second set rolls into classic jam sequences and a powerful St. Stephen/Not Fade Away medley, delivering the typical 1971 arc of high energy to psychedelic climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry’s guitar work is praised overall; a broken string during Rider is noted but his snarl on Sugar Magnolia gets kudos.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil steps up with lead‑bass lines and even belts a solo after the broken string incident, receiving positive remarks.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s vocal contribution highlighted when he sings the ‘headlight’ line in Rider; generally mentioned as solid.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums are described as “kicking royal A**!!!”; one reviewer questions a 14‑second isolated drum track but overall rhythm section is praised.
